name: La Peruyal, Cangas de Onís, La Cuevona – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Parres, nestled in Asturias, Spain, offer diverse terrain for cycling enthusiasts. The region features rugged mountains like Pico Pienzu and the Sierra del Sueve, providing significant elevation changes and panoramic views. Valleys carved by rivers such as the Sella and Piloña, alongside extensive Atlantic meadows, create a varied landscape of forest trails and open paths. Its proximity to the Picos de Europa National Park further contributes to challenging routes with limestone features and high-mountain tracks. Best mountain bike trails around Parres The most popular mountain bike trail is La Peruyal, Cangas de Onís, La Cuevona – ruta circular, a difficult 31.7 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route features significant climbs and offers expansive views of the surrounding Asturian landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Antolín de Bedón Beach – Queen's Viewpoint loop from Arriondas, a difficult 85.9 miles (138.2 km) path. This extensive route combines coastal scenery with inland climbs, providing a long and challenging ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Santa Marina Beach – Santa Marina Beach loop from Arriondas, a 24.9 miles (40.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas and varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Parres is defined by rugged mountains, verdant river valleys, and Atlantic meadows. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult, technical descents. The routes in Parres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Parres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Parres?
A: Parres offers a selection of 16 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 9 moderate routes and 7 more challenging, difficult trails, ensuring a diverse experience for all riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Parres?
A: The mountain bike trails in Parres feature a diverse landscape, from rugged mountains like Pico Pienzu and the Sierra del Sueve with significant elevation changes, to verdant river valleys carved by the Sella and Piloña. You'll also encounter extensive Atlantic meadows and forest trails, with some routes offering technical descents and limestone features, especially closer to the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for families in Parres?
A: While many trails in Parres are moderate to difficult, there are options that can be adapted for families depending on experience. For a less strenuous ride, consider exploring the Atlantic meadows and valley tracks which tend to be less technical than the high-mountain routes. Always check the difficulty and elevation profiles on komoot before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Parres?
A: Many natural areas in Parres, including trails, are generally dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Always check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas, and ensure your dog is fit enough for the ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Parres?
A: Yes, Parres offers several circular mountain bike routes. For example, you could try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027483183/la-peruyal-cangas-de-onis-la-cuevona-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Peruyal, Cangas de Onís, La Cuevona – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 50.9 km loop with significant climbs, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marina Beach – Santa Marina Beach loop from Arriondas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 40 km coastal and varied terrain ride.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or natural features to see while mountain biking in Parres?
A: Parres offers stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can experience breathtaking panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1029177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pienzu Peak&lt;/a&gt; in the Sierra del Sueve, which overlooks the coast and the Picos de Europa. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fito&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4770933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canónigos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The Praderas de Bustaco also offer spectacular natural scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Parres?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Parres is generally from spring through autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically more stable and pleasant, making for ideal riding conditions. Summer can be warm, but the coastal breezes and shaded forest trails offer relief. Always check local weather forecasts before your ride.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Parres?
A: Yes, Arriondas, the capital of Parres, is a hub for active tourism and offers various amenities. You'll find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options there. Many routes start or pass through Arriondas, making it a convenient base for your mountain biking adventures.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Parres?
A: The mountain bike trails in Parres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic valley paths, and the stunning views of both the coast and the Picos de Europa. The variety of routes ensures there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails in Parres for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Parres is known for its challenging routes, especially those that venture into the rugged mountains and closer to the Picos de Europa National Park. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10505841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antolín de Bedón Beach – Queen's Viewpoint loop from Arriondas&lt;/a&gt;, an extensive 138.2 km route, offer significant elevation gains and technical sections, requiring good fitness and experience.

Q: What other activities can I do in Parres besides mountain biking?
A: Parres is an excellent destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Beyond mountain biking, you can enjoy canoeing on the famous Sella River, hiking through the Picos de Europa National Park, or exploring the numerous trails for general cycling. Other adventure sports available include canyoneering, climbing, horseback riding, and rafting. The region also has cultural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/492581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covadonga Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, Parres offers several moderate mountain bike trails perfect for intermediate riders. Routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arriondas – The Cuevona of Cueves loop from Arriondas&lt;/a&gt; (32.3 km) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10505547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arriondas – Views of the Roman bridge loop from Arriondas&lt;/a&gt; (28.5 km) provide engaging rides with manageable climbs and descents, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without extreme technical demands.
tips: The Roman bridge of Cangas de Onís is located over the Sella River, separating the municipalities of Cangas de Onís and Parres. The truth is that the current bridge is not Roman, but medieval from the 13th century (although restored on several occasions), but it was built on the foundations of a Roman bridge. The bridge has become a symbol of Asturias, since the cross on the inverted crescent commemorates the defeat of the Arabs in the battle of Covadonga.

The houses of the Indianos have beautiful buildings with a very distinguishable architecture and beautiful colors. The Indians were emigrants who made their fortune in Latin America (Cuba and Mexico, especially). It is also worth seeing the Church of the Holy Cross, the Roman bridge, the Aula del Reino de Asturias, the Parish Church, the Pintu Palace, the Market Square and the Dago House.

One of the few natural cavities that can be crossed by road. It gives access to the town of Cuevas del Agua. For 300 winding meters you can see magnificent stalactites, stalagmites and other formations with names such as "La lengua del diablo" or "Las barbas de Santiago"
